resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, sensitivity result, symptom explanation, or diet recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but it does not diagnose food sensitivity, food allergy, celiac disease, symptoms, immune response, alcohol metabolism, or caffeine metabolism.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated food-sensitivity model, food allergy evidence, celiac evidence, immune-response evidence, symptom context, elimination-diet model, lab context, substance-metabolism model, or ancestry-applicability metadata is supplied with this fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The local run can preserve observed sample report structure and explain limitations, but it should not diagnose food sensitivity or recommend elimination diets without validated clinical evidence.

resultRows.item: Alcohol, caffeine, lactose, and gluten summary cards
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample summary cards show Alcohol: Slow Metabolism; Caffeine: Fast Metabolism; Lactose: Likely Sensitive; Gluten: Not Likely Sensitive, with a legend separating likely-sensitive from not-likely-sensitive labels. The sample summary page presents the four result cards before detailed substance and sensitivity sections.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample introduces alcohol and caffeine as substances whose metabolism can vary with genetic markers, then notes that slower metabolism markers may mean the body takes longer to clear alcohol or caffeine. The substances section precedes the detailed Alcohol Metabolism and Caffeine Metabolism pages.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: ADH1C gene table and slow-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Alcohol Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have slow alcohol metabolism. The visible gene table lists ADH1C marker GP000071 genotype TT with slow alcohol metabolism impact, plus ADH1C GP000072 genotype CC and ADH1C GP000073 genotype TT with normal alcohol metabolism impact. The page also lists common alcohol sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking drinks, spacing them out, and drinking water.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: CYP1A2 gene table and fast-metabolism result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Caffeine Metabolism page says the sample genome is more likely to have fast caffeine metabolism. The visible gene table lists CYP1A2 marker GP000071 genotype TT with fast caffeine metabolism impact. The page lists common caffeine sources such as coffee, tea, soft drinks, energy drinks, chocolate, certain medications, and yerba mate, plus a sleep-timing action block.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: Food sensitivity versus food allergy explanation
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Sensitivities section distinguishes food sensitivity from food allergy, explains that sensitivities can involve difficulty digesting components such as lactose or gluten, and names non-genetic contributors such as gut microbiome imbalance, gastrointestinal disease, stress, age, additives, sleep, and physical inactivity. The section explicitly says genetics can play a role but do not work in isolation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: HLA-DQA1 and HLA-DQ8 gene table with not-likely-sensitive result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Gluten Sensitivity page says the sample genome is not likely sensitive to gluten. The visible gene table lists HLA-DQA1 marker GP000071 genotype TT and HLA-DQ8 marker GP000072 genotype CC, both with not-likely-sensitive-to-gluten impact. The page lists common gluten sources and includes a what-can-you-do block about a temporary gluten-removal trial.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.item: LCT and MCM6-LCT gene table with likely-sensitive result
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Lactose Sensitivity page says the sample genome is more likely to be sensitive to lactose. The visible gene table lists LCT marker GP000071 genotype TT with more-likely-sensitive-to-lactose impact and MCM6-LCT marker GP000072 genotype CC with less-likely-sensitive-to-lactose impact. The page lists common lactose sources and a what-can-you-do block about tracking dairy reactions, considering lower-lactose foods, and discussing dairy reduction or lactose enzymes with a healthcare practitioner.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample includes a complimentary dietitian consultation page offering a free one-on-one registered dietitian consultation and a QR-code scheduling callout. This page is part of the observed commercial report structure and is not a local recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed public Food Sensitivity sample-report structure only. Keep this separate from local genome evidence; it is not a personal alcohol, caffeine, lactose, gluten, allergy, celiac, symptom, elimination-diet, supplement, medical, or treatment recommendation.

foodContext: Use this output as Food Sensitivity sample-format replication and educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a food-sensitivity diagnosis.
Discuss symptoms, food history, allergy testing, celiac testing, labs, alcohol or caffeine concerns, and elimination diets with qualified professionals when relevant.
Do not remove foods, add supplements, change caffeine or alcohol use for medical reasons, or change medical care based on genotype or sample-only rows.

genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture supplies a derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 call for lactase-persistence context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Report as educational lactose-related marker context only; do not diagnose lactose intolerance, food sensitivity, allergy, celiac disease, symptoms, or prescribe diet changes from genotype alone.

genotypeSummary.genotype: calibrated food-sensitivity, food-allergy, celiac, immune-response, symptom, elimination-diet, lab, diet-history, substance-metabolism, and ancestry applicability models unavailable
genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture intentionally omits calibrated sensitivity models and non-genetic context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Mark allergy, celiac, sensitivity, symptom, elimination-diet, substance-metabolism, lab, supplement, and treatment sections unavailable instead of inferring them.

genotypeSummary.effect: Sample alcohol metabolism page shows one slow-metabolism impact and two normal-metabolism impacts.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not treat the sample markers as local alcohol-metabolism evidence.

genotypeSummary.effect: Sample caffeine page shows fast caffeine metabolism impact.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not treat the sample marker as local caffeine-metabolism evidence.

genotypeSummary.effect: Sample gluten page shows not-likely-sensitive-to-gluten impacts.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not treat the sample markers as local gluten-sensitivity or celiac evidence.

genotypeSummary.effect: Sample lactose page shows one more-likely-sensitive and one less-likely-sensitive impact.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample-report structure only; do not treat the sample markers as local lactose-intolerance or symptom evidence.

missingInputs: Authenticated Sequencing.com marketplace detail-page extraction is still pending.
No calibrated food-sensitivity, food-allergy, celiac, immune-response, symptom, elimination-diet, lab, substance-metabolism, or ancestry-applicability model is supplied.
Diet history, symptom timing, clinical testing, medication context, alcohol/caffeine exposure, and non-genetic contributors are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Food symptoms and sensitivities can involve immune, digestive, dietary, environmental, medication, stress, sleep, and other non-genetic factors.
A lactose-related marker cannot explain broad food sensitivity, allergy, celiac disease, alcohol metabolism, caffeine metabolism, or symptom patterns.
The sample PDF rows are observed example output only and must not be treated as local-user results.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not diagnose food sensitivity, food allergy, celiac disease, lactose intolerance, symptoms, immune response, alcohol tolerance, or caffeine tolerance, and does not prescribe elimination diets.
Sample-report recommendations, common-source lists, and consultation callouts are preserved as format examples only, not local advice.
Authenticated Sequencing.com detail-page extraction is still pending.
